issue_title: File write action — sequencer writes parsed files to disk
issue_description: New write_files: bool field on PhaseDefinition. When set, sequencer parses output using output_parser (from #188) and writes files to working_dir. Records files_written metadata. Dry-run mode logs but doesn't write. Safety: refuse writes outside base_dir.
acceptance_criteria: New field write_files: bool = False on PhaseDefinition dataclass
YAML loader parses write_files from phase config
When write_files=True, sequencer calls output_parser.parse_output() on phase result
Parsed files are written to output_dir/phase_id/ (or working_dir if configured)
Phase result metadata includes files_written: list of paths that were written
Dry-run mode: logs file paths and content length but does NOT write to disk
Safety: refuse to write outside base_dir (path traversal protection from output_parser)
Existing files are overwritten with a warning log
Tests cover: write_files=True, write_files=False (default), dry-run, path traversal rejection, directory creation
codebase_context: Depends on output_parser.py from #188 (parse_output, extract_and_write). The sequencer (sequencer.py) stores phase results in self.phase_outputs. After _execute_and_wait() returns, if write_files is True, the sequencer should parse the output text and write files. PhaseDefinition is in templates.py. The YAML loader is in TemplateEngine.load_template(). The pipeline output directory is available in self.pipeline_context.
files_to_modify: src/orchestration_engine/templates.py — add write_files field to PhaseDefinition + YAML parsing
src/orchestration_engine/sequencer.py — add file writing logic after phase execution
tests/test_output_parser.py — add extract_and_write tests if not already covered
constraints: Must import from output_parser module (created in #188)
write_files defaults to False for backward compatibility
File writes happen AFTER the phase result is stored in phase_outputs
Dry-run detection: check if executor is DryRunExecutor or if a dry_run flag exists
